title: git-semver: Add tag subcommand and make --no-push skip tagging
body:
## Summary

Add a new `tag` subcommand to `git-semver` and change `--no-push` behavior on `bump`/`bump-all` to also skip tag creation.

## Context

Protected branch support requires splitting bump and tag/release into separate phases. Currently tags are created during `bump`/`bump-all` and there's no way to create tags independently from an already-bumped commit.

## Acceptance Criteria

### `tag` subcommand
- [ ] `git-semver tag` reads VERSION file(s) from config (root + all subdirectories)
- [ ] Creates annotated version tags (`vX.Y.Z` for root, `<subdir>/vX.Y.Z` for subdirs)
- [ ] Creates/updates `latest` tag (`git tag -f latest`)
- [ ] `--push` flag pushes tags (`git push --tags --force`)
- [ ] Without `--push`, tags are created locally only
- [ ] Supports `--config <path>` and `--subdir <name>` flags (consistent with other subcommands)
- [ ] Idempotent: if version tag already exists at HEAD, no error

### `--no-push` behavior change
- [ ] `bump --no-push` skips both push AND tag creation (currently only skips push)
- [ ] `bump-all --no-push` same behavior
- [ ] `--no-commit` continues to skip commit, tag, and push (unchanged)

### Tests
- [ ] Test `tag` subcommand: creates correct tags from VERSION files
- [ ] Test `tag` with subdirectories: creates `<subdir>/vX.Y.Z` tags
- [ ] Test `tag --push`: pushes tags
- [ ] Test `tag` idempotency: no error when tag exists at HEAD
- [ ] Test `bump --no-push` no longer creates tags
- [ ] Test `bump-all --no-push` no longer creates tags

### Docs
- [ ] Update `git-semver` `--help` output to include `tag` subcommand
- [ ] Add `tag` to the Commands section of README.md

title: semver: Refactor bump-and-release into semver bump with protected branch fallback
body:
## Summary

Rename `bump-and-release` to `semver` and refactor the bump flow into a `bump` subcommand that detects protected branches and falls back to PR mode automatically.

## Context

Currently `bump-and-release` is a monolithic script. It needs to become `semver` with subcommands. The `bump` subcommand always uses `git-semver bump-all --no-push` (which now skips tags too, per gsv-14cf.1), then attempts `git push`. If push fails due to branch protection (`GH006` / "protected branch"), it falls back to creating a PR.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Rename
- [ ] Rename `bump-and-release` → `semver` (file name)
- [ ] Update `install.sh` to install `semver` instead of `bump-and-release`
- [ ] Update `version-bump.yml` template to call `semver` instead of `bump-and-release`
- [ ] Update any references in README, config, tests

### `semver bump` subcommand
- [ ] Handles both push and dispatch events (existing event routing logic)
- [ ] Always calls `git-semver bump-all --no-push` (push events) or `git-semver bump <type> --no-push` (dispatch)
- [ ] After bump commit, attempts `git push` to default branch
- [ ] On success: exits (release phase handled separately)
- [ ] On protected branch failure (stderr contains `GH006` or `protected branch`): falls back to PR mode (create branch, push branch, create PR)
- [ ] On other push failure: exits with error
- [ ] `sync_branch()` still runs before bump
- [ ] Config reading (`on_merge`, `automerge`) still respected — `automerge: false` skips the push attempt entirely and goes straight to PR
- [ ] Calling `semver` with no subcommand prints usage

### Tests
- [ ] Test successful push path (unprotected branch)
- [ ] Test protected branch detection and PR fallback
- [ ] Test `automerge: false` goes directly to PR mode (no push attempt)
- [ ] Test non-protection push failure raises error
- [ ] Test dispatch event routing
- [ ] Test existing push event routing
- [ ] Update all existing `bump-and-release` tests for new script name and subcommand structure

### Docs
- [ ] Update README references from `bump-and-release` to `semver`

title: semver: Add publish subcommand (tag + GitHub releases)
body:
## Summary

Add a `publish` subcommand to `semver` that creates version tags and GitHub releases. This is the second phase of the two-phase protected branch flow.

## Context

After a bump commit lands on main (either via direct push or merged PR), `semver publish` runs to create tags and GitHub releases. It calls `git-semver tag --push` then creates GitHub releases for each version tag.

## Acceptance Criteria

### `semver publish` subcommand
- [ ] Calls `git-semver tag --push` to create and push version tags
- [ ] Creates GitHub releases for each version tag at HEAD (via `gh release create <tag> --generate-notes`)
- [ ] Skips the `latest` tag when creating releases (only version tags get releases)
- [ ] Handles monorepo: creates releases for all component tags (root + subdirectories)
- [ ] Squash merge support: tags are created at HEAD (the squash commit on main), not the original branch commit — this works naturally since `git-semver tag` reads VERSION and tags HEAD

### Edge cases
- [ ] If no VERSION file changes are detected (e.g., non-bump commit somehow triggers this), exits cleanly with no-op
- [ ] If `git-semver tag` fails (e.g., tag already exists not at HEAD), surfaces error clearly

### Tests
- [ ] Test publish calls `git-semver tag --push` then creates releases
- [ ] Test publish with multiple components (monorepo)
- [ ] Test publish skips `latest` tag for release creation
- [ ] Test publish no-ops gracefully when no version tags are created

### Docs
- [ ] Add `semver publish` to README workflow documentation

title: version-bump.yml: Two-job workflow (bump + publish)
body:
## Summary

Refactor the `version-bump.yml` workflow template to have two mutually exclusive jobs: `bump` and `publish`.

## Context

Protected branch support requires splitting the workflow into two phases. When a non-bump commit lands on main, the `bump` job runs. When a bump commit lands (either direct push or merged PR), the `publish` job runs to create tags and releases. The two jobs are mutually exclusive via their `if` conditions, preventing loops.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Bump job
- [ ] Runs on push to main/master when commit message does NOT start with `chore: bump version` or `chore: install`
- [ ] Runs on `workflow_dispatch`
- [ ] Calls `semver bump` (replaces current `bump-and-release` call)
- [ ] Passes same environment variables (GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_EVENT_BEFORE, INPUT_* for dispatch)

### Publish job
- [ ] Runs on push to main/master ONLY when commit message starts with `chore: bump version`
- [ ] Calls `semver publish`
- [ ] Passes GH_TOKEN and repo context
- [ ] Checkout with `fetch-depth: 0` and `fetch-tags: true`

### Loop prevention
- [ ] Bump and publish jobs are mutually exclusive (one or the other per push event, never both)
- [ ] `chore: install` commits still skipped by both jobs

### Squash merge handling
- [ ] Squash merges preserve `chore: bump version` prefix in commit message (GitHub uses PR title as squash message)
- [ ] Publish job triggers correctly for squash-merged bump PRs (e.g., `chore: bump version v0.0.18 (#42)`)

### Tests
- [ ] Test workflow template renders correct `if` conditions for both jobs
- [ ] Test that bump and publish conditions are mutually exclusive

### Docs
- [ ] Update README workflow section to describe two-job architecture
- [ ] Document that protected branches work automatically (no config needed)

## Notes

The `install.sh` template rendering needs to be updated to produce the new two-job workflow. Existing consumer repos will get the new workflow on next vendor update.

title: Docs: Update README for protected branch support
body:
## Summary

Update README.md to document that protected branches work out of the box and explain the two-phase architecture.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document that protected branches are supported automatically (no special config)
- [ ] Explain the two-phase flow: bump job tries direct push, falls back to PR; publish job creates tags + releases after merge
- [ ] Update the `git-semver` Commands section to include the `tag` subcommand
- [ ] Update references from `bump-and-release` to `semver` throughout
- [ ] Update the GitHub Actions section to describe the two-job workflow (bump + publish)
- [ ] Update the "What gets installed" tree to show `semver` instead of `bump-and-release`
- [ ] Update the File Classification table
- [ ] Update the `automerge` config key docs to note it's now less relevant (protected branches auto-detected)

title: Missing workflow template YAML tests for two-job if conditions
Stale bump-and-release references in vendored files, manifests, and templates
git-semver tag fails with confusing error when tag exists at different commit
Bump job should tag and release inline when direct push succeeds (unprotected branches)
body:
## Problem

The version-bump workflow splits into two jobs: `bump` and `publish`. On **unprotected branches**, the bump job pushes directly with `GITHUB_TOKEN`. GitHub Actions policy prevents `GITHUB_TOKEN` pushes from triggering new workflow runs (to avoid infinite loops). So the publish job never fires and **no tags or releases are created**.

### Impact

Any repo with an unprotected main branch gets VERSION file bumps but no git tags or GitHub releases. Consumers that resolve versions from tags/releases (e.g., `.vendored/install`) see stale versions.

## Fix

In `release`, both `handle_push_bump()` and `handle_dispatch_bump()`: when `try_push_or_pr()` returns `True` (direct push succeeded, meaning unprotected branch), tag and release in the same job.

```python
pushed = try_push_or_pr(commit_title)
if pushed:
    # Unprotected branch — publish job won't fire, so tag here
    git_semver("tag", "--push")
    create_releases()
```

If `try_push_or_pr()` returns `False` (fell back to PR), do nothing — the publish job handles tagging when the PR merges.

The workflow YAML doesn't need changes — the fix is entirely in the `release` script.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After a direct push bump on an unprotected branch, tags and GitHub releases are created in the same workflow run
- [ ] Protected branch flow (PR fallback -> merge -> publish job) still works unchanged
- [ ] `workflow_dispatch` bump also tags correctly on unprotected branches
- [ ] Tests cover both paths: direct push -> immediate tag+release, PR fallback -> deferred tag+release

## Scope

Only the `release` script (lines ~168 and ~203). No workflow YAML changes needed.
